//: job-scout platform v4.0.0 — zero-prompt permissions for unattended AND interactive scans (any profile), plus the session lifecycle hooks.
//install: On the machine that runs the scans: copy this file to .claude/settings.json at the REPO ROOT (mkdir -p .claude && cp claude-settings.example.json .claude/settings.json). Re-copy after engine updates. Your own 'Yes, don't ask again' clicks accumulate separately in .claude/settings.local.json and are preserved. NOTE: this file must stay in sync with the committed .claude/settings.json — if you change hooks or permissions in one, mirror it in the other in the same PR.
//scope: Scoped to the scan workflow: core script execution (scripts do their own outbound HTTP), read-only shell inspection of results, git operations for state_sync, WebFetch/WebSearch for the judgment layer's occasional direct look at a posting, state writes under profiles/*/state only. No blanket skip-permissions flag; the cron fallback (claude -p) may additionally need --allowedTools set to the same list — a broader flag like --dangerously-skip-permissions trades safety for convenience and is NOT the default.
//hooks: SessionStart installs core/'s Python deps AND injects docs/STATE.md + git status into context (the session dashboard — see CLAUDE.md Session START protocol). Stop nudges the Definition-of-Done wrap-up: GUIDED-FLOW prompt fold-in and the docs/STATE.md + PROGRESS.md state update.
